_meta.description: Product roadmap source of truth for Maintenance Agent. Edit custom_items[] to add user stories and tasks. Feature statuses are auto-detected from the codebase.

project.name: Maintenance Agent
project.subtitle: Full-stack property maintenance platform — Swiss market

phases.name: Foundation Hardening
phases.goal: Clear the critical path blockers before adding any new features. Three items in your backlog directly block monetizable work.

phases.name: Cashflow Layer
phases.goal: Build the financial visibility layer for building owners — cash in, cash out, per-building balance — without crossing into accounting. Anchored on BuildingFinancialSnapshot.

phases.name: Private Landlord SaaS
phases.goal: The current product is built for professional managers. This phase creates a simplified entry point for private individuals owning 1–20 properties — same backend, different UX skin and onboarding.

phases.name: AI Triage Upgrade
phases.goal: Extend the existing triage service from classify-and-route to resolve-before-dispatch. The infrastructure is there. The intelligence layer is not.

phases.name: NOI Intelligence
phases.goal: Connect cashflow + maintenance analytics + vacancy data into a per-building P&L proxy. Explicitly not accounting. The output is decision intelligence, not a financial statement.

phases.name: ESG Layer
phases.goal: Energy tracking, cantonal compliance deadlines, retrofit ROI modeling. New vertical targeting institutional owners.

features.title: Email Delivery (SMTP / SendGrid)
features.description: EmailOutbox model and dev sink are built. Wire a real transport. Without this, rent reminders, invoice delivery, and tenant notifications are silent.

slices.title: Wire SMTP transport
acceptance_criteria: real SMTP transport is configured
notificationService can send via production transport
slices.prompt_template: Implement production email transport using existing EmailOutbox and notificationService patterns.

validation.notes: nodemailer wired; dev console sink when SMTP_HOST unset; 20 unit tests all pass

slices.title: Configure SMTP env vars
acceptance_criteria: SMTP_HOST env var is set in .env
SMTP_PORT, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS are configured
slices.prompt_template: Add SMTP configuration environment variables and update .env.example.

slices.title: Integration test for email delivery
acceptance_criteria: test verifies email sent through SMTP transport
test covers failure/retry scenario

features.title: Legal DSL Variable Resolver
features.description: LegalVariable, LegalVariableVersion, LegalRule are all in schema. Wire the resolver to live data. Reference interest rate ingestion first as proof of concept.

features.title: Skribble e-Sign Integration
features.description: SignatureRequest model exists. Digital lease signing is table stakes for private landlord positioning. Skribble is the Swiss-native choice (QES compliant).

features.title: Route Layer Refactor (CQ-1/3/4)
features.description: legal.ts, tenants.ts, leases.ts have business logic in route handlers. Extract to services before P1 financial work adds more surface area.

features.title: QR-Invoice Rent Demand Generation
features.description: Generate Swiss QR-invoices (ISO 20022 compliant) for monthly rent demands from lease data. Send via email. Track payment status automatically.

features.title: MT940 Bank Statement Import
features.description: Accept MT940/CSV bank exports from all major Swiss banks. Auto-match incoming payments to open rent demands by amount + reference. Flag unmatched.

features.title: BuildingFinancialSnapshot Engine
features.description: The model exists. Build the service that populates it: monthly snapshot of income, expenses, vacancy loss, net balance. Central data structure for P4.

features.title: Arrears Management + Automated Reminders
features.description: Escalating reminder workflow: friendly nudge (D+3) → formal notice (D+14) → Betreibungshinweis template (D+30). All emails sent via EmailOutbox.

features.title: Nebenkostenabrechnung Generator
features.description: Annual utility charge statement generation. Input: expense invoices tagged as Nebenkosten over 12 months. Output: PDF per tenant showing their share. OR Art. 257a/b compliant.

features.title: Owner Cashflow Dashboard
features.description: Extend /owner/financials into a real cashflow dashboard. Per-building monthly view: income bar, expense bar, net balance line. Maintenance events shown as expense spikes.

features.title: Self-Service Onboarding Wizard
features.description: Private landlord operational in under 15 minutes: add building → add unit → upload/create lease → set rent → generate first QR-invoice. Multi-language from day one.

features.title: Owner-as-Manager Unified Mode
features.description: OrgConfig flag that collapses Manager and Owner personas into a single simplified interface for private landlords who manage their own properties.

features.title: Multilingual Tenant Portal (FR/DE/IT)
features.description: All tenant-facing pages serve FR/DE/IT based on tenant language preference. Without this, the platform is only usable in monolingual cantons.

features.title: Canton-Aware Initial Rent Notice (Formular B)
features.description: Generate the correct canton-specific initial rent notification form from lease data, pre-filled, ready to sign. First tangible proof of legal layer value.

features.title: PWA / Mobile-Optimized Tenant Portal
features.description: Wrap tenant portal as a PWA — installable on iOS/Android, push notifications, offline document access. Replaces phone calls for a private landlord’s tenant.

features.title: Document Vault
features.description: Structured document storage — lease PDFs, signed addenda, Nebenkosten statements, correspondence — linked to tenant and property, searchable, exportable.

features.title: Photo + Video Evidence on Intake
features.description: Tenant attaches photos/video when submitting a request. Forwarded to contractor before dispatch — eliminates ‘I need to assess first’ callout.

features.title: LLM Classification + Urgency Scoring
features.description: Replace/augment rule-based triage with LLM-based classification. Input: free-text + photos. Output: category, urgency score (1–5), suggested contractor type, legal obligation flag.

features.title: Guided Troubleshooting Flows
features.description: When tenant submits a request, present structured Q&A flow to resolve at intake without dispatch. Target: resolve ~20% without sending a contractor.

features.title: Maintenance Analytics Dashboard
features.description: Requests by category/building, avg time-to-resolution, self-resolution rate, contractor performance, cost-per-category. Resolves the reports.js backlog item.

features.title: Maintenance → Cost Attribution Engine
features.description: Link every closed maintenance request to its total cost. Tag as capex or opex. Maintenance events appear as tagged expense events on building cashflow timeline.

features.title: Vacancy Impact Quantification
features.description: Calculate and display vacancy cost as a negative cash event. Link to reason where known. Makes the cost of slow re-letting viscerally clear.

features.title: NOI Proxy Dashboard
features.description: Per-building: gross income, total expenses (with maintenance breakdown), vacancy loss, net operating income proxy. Clearly labelled indicative. Year-on-year comparison.

features.title: Proactive Asset Health Signals
features.description: Use AssetIntervention + DepreciationStandard to surface forward-looking signals: aging assets, recommended replacement budget, projected future expense.

features.title: Treuhänder / Accountant Export Package
features.description: One-click annual summary export for accountant: all income, expense invoices, Nebenkosten statements, lease periods, vacancy periods. Abacus CSV format.

features.title: bLink Open Banking Feed
features.description: Replace MT940 manual import with live bank feed via SIX bLink. Automatic daily reconciliation. Build only after MT940 is proven and trusted.

we_own: Request lifecycle (triage → approval → job → invoice → payment)
Legal decision engine (DSL rules, canton scoping, category mapping)
Rental application pipeline (apply → screen → approve → lease)
grey_zone: Email delivery (outbox exists, no SMTP provider wired)
Payment reconciliation (QR-bill generated, no eBill/bank feed)
Push notifications (in-app works, no mobile push)
we_dont_own: Formal accounting (VAT, balance sheet, P&L — belongs in Abacus/Sage)
LLM triage (rule-based only, no OpenAI integration)
Vision-based damage classification (image upload exists via MaintenanceAttachment, no AI analysis)
GEAK energy ratings (no Swiss government API integration)

intake_items.raw_text: Roll out H1 auth wrappers (withAuthRequired/withRole) to remaining ~100 unprotected routes. Currently only 7 routes in config.ts + 25 GET endpoints are wrapped. The remaining mutation endpoints (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) across requests.ts, invoices.ts, leases.ts, inventory.ts, legal.ts, and rentalApplications.ts still use ad-hoc auth or no auth. This is a security hardening task, not a behavior change.
intake_items.source: EPIC_HISTORY.md — H1 next steps + Project Audit Mar 4
intake_items.triage_notes: Mechanical refactor: wrap ~100 route handlers in withAuthRequired/withRole. No behavior change.

intake_items.raw_text: Implement summary DTOs for requests, invoices, and leases (H5 expansion). JobSummaryDTO already exists with view=summary parameter. Same pattern needs to be applied to: MaintenanceRequestSummaryDTO (partially exists but list endpoint doesn't use it), InvoiceSummaryDTO (interface exists in api-client but not wired), LeaseSummaryDTO (doesn't exist yet). Reduces overfetch on list endpoints.
intake_items.triage_notes: Unparked. Replicate JobSummaryDTO pattern to Request, Invoice, Lease. Mechanical.

intake_items.raw_text: Create /manager/people/owners page — page referenced in ManagerSidebar nav config but doesn't exist yet. Should list all OWNER-role users in the org, show which buildings they own (via BuildingOwner junction), and allow managing ownership assignments. Similar to the existing tenants and contractors lists.
intake_items.triage_notes: Large scope — executable but may benefit from sub-tasks.

intake_items.raw_text: legal.ts route-layer violation (A-5 / CQ-5): 19 direct Prisma calls in routes/legal.ts. This is the worst-offending route file. Needs refactoring to use repository pattern (legalRepository.ts) and move orchestration to workflows. Related CQ-1 through CQ-15 cover other route files with smaller violations. legal.ts is the highest priority because it has the most violations and touches the most complex domain.
intake_items.triage_notes: Medium scope — single ticket, may need detailed acceptance criteria.

intake_items.raw_text: Request.orgId migration (H6) — add orgId column to Request model for direct org filtering instead of FK traversal. Currently resolved dynamically via resolveRequestOrg() which walks unit→building→org chain. 7-step migration plan documented: add nullable → backfill via SQL → make required → update queries → keep resolvers for validation. Deferred until multi-org launch or performance bottleneck. Estimated effort: 2-3 hours.
intake_items.triage_notes: Large scope — executable but may benefit from sub-tasks.

intake_items.raw_text: LegalVariable values not wired into DSL condition evaluation — the LegalDecisionEngine evaluates DSL conditions (always_true, category_match, estimated_cost_above, asset_age_above_pct, etc.) but does not consume LegalVariable records. Variables are stored in the DB (LegalVariable + LegalVariableVersion) with versioning, but the DSL evaluator never reads them. This means variable-based conditions can't be used in rules. Referenced in copilot-instructions.md known issues and LKDE epic. Needs: wire variable resolution into evaluateCondition(), add variable_equals/variable_above DSL operators.
intake_items.triage_notes: Epic scope with unresolved prerequisites — park until dependencies are met.

intake_items.raw_text: LegalDecisionEngine canton-specific filtering for DSL evaluator — currently the ingestion pipeline respects LegalSource scope (FEDERAL + canton), but the DSL evaluator itself does not filter rules by canton. All rules are evaluated regardless of the request's building canton. Needs: canton field on LegalRule or LegalRuleVersion, filter evaluated rules by canton before DSL evaluation. Noted as 'separate future slice' in LegalSource Scope epic.
intake_items.triage_notes: Add canton field to LegalRule, filter evaluated rules by building canton in DSL evaluator.

intake_items.raw_text: Production email provider integration — EmailOutbox currently uses dev sink (emails queued in DB, viewable via GET /__dev/email-outbox). Need to integrate a real SMTP/SendGrid provider for production. Related to F-P0-001 Email Delivery. The EmailOutbox model + template system + queue logic already exist, just need the send adapter. Candidate rejection emails (INT-024), overdue invoice notifications (INT-032), lease signing feedback already generate outbox records.
intake_items.source: EPIC_HISTORY.md — Rental Applications epic + email outbox references
intake_items.triage_notes: Attaches to F-P0-001 Email Delivery. EmailOutbox + templates exist, need send adapter.

intake_items.raw_text: Formalize background job scheduler — processSelectionTimeouts() and processAttachmentRetention() currently run on hourly interval started in server.ts via setInterval. No formal scheduler, no retry logic, no dead letter, no monitoring. For production: need at minimum a proper scheduling mechanism (node-cron or pg-boss), execution logging, failure alerting. Currently also available via POST /__dev/rental/run-jobs (dev only).
intake_items.source: EPIC_HISTORY.md — Rental Applications epic background jobs section
intake_items.triage_notes: Replace setInterval with node-cron or pg-boss. Add execution logging. Medium effort.

intake_items.raw_text: Fix 28 pre-existing server-spawn test timeouts — multiple test suites (auth.manager-gates, tenantSession, ia, contracts, rentalContracts, rentEstimation) have server-spawn-based integration tests that intermittently timeout under Jest load. Root cause: each test suite spawns a full server process via tsx, and under parallel Jest workers + CI resource constraints, startup can exceed 15-30s timeouts. Possible fixes: shared server instance across suites, in-process testing with supertest, or increase CI resources.
intake_items.source: EPIC_HISTORY.md — multiple test fix sections + verification tables
intake_items.triage_notes: Consolidate to shared test server or supertest. Systematic fix across 6 suites.

intake_items.raw_text: Remaining route-level layer violations (CQ-1 through CQ-15 minus resolved) — routes/legal.ts (19 Prisma calls, worst offender), routes/rentalApplications.ts (direct service calls bypassing workflows for some endpoints), routes/inventory.ts (mixed pattern, some use repos, some don't). Each should be refactored to: routes → workflows → services → repositories. No behavior changes, structural only.
intake_items.source: EPIC_HISTORY.md — Architecture Hardening + AUDIT.md CQ findings
intake_items.triage_notes: Attaches to F-P0-004 Route Layer Refactor. Covers rentalApplications.ts + inventory.ts.

intake_items.raw_text: 3 proxy routes retain custom logic and can't use proxyToBackend() — requests.js (field rename on POST), contractors.js (multi-path routing), requests/approve.js (method + path transform). These are the last 3 of 94 total proxy routes not migrated to the shared helper. Document their custom logic in comments and consider if the backend can be adjusted to eliminate the need for client-side transforms.
intake_items.source: EPIC_HISTORY.md — Project Audit Mar 4, H3 section
intake_items.triage_notes: Small scope — one focused ticket.

intake_items.title: Invoice review
intake_items.raw_text: all invoices created/received should be viewable within the UI directly, wether on tenant, contractor, manager or owner side. We should have a standard template that shows on the top left the invoice emitter details, below that on the right the receiver details, underneath that a date and place, then the breakdown of the invoice, total, and at the bottom the swiss banking compatible QR code that enables users to scan and pay.
intake_items.triage_notes: Epic-sized item. Needs decomposition into smaller tickets before execution.
